title: carex littledalei
intro:
Carex littledalei, also known as Little Dale's sedge, is a species of flowering plant in the family Cyperaceae, which is commonly found in the mountainous regions of New Zealand.

Carex littledalei has been used in traditional Maori medicine for its medicinal properties, which are believed to have anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects. The plant is rich in flavonoids and other bioactive compounds that may help to alleviate symptoms of respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma. In terms of health benefits, Carex littledalei may help to reduce inflammation, improve immune function, and provide relief from coughs and congestion. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used in teas, infusions, and tinctures to harness its medicinal properties.
